metadata.title: Healthcare Industry Phishing and Malware Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple Healthcare professionals targeted with spearphishing emails and exploited using drive-by compromises, leading to malware execution and data exfiltration.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Command, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command Execution (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send targeted phishing emails to healthcare professionals, tricking them into revealing sensitive information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in outdated software on the healthcare organization's website to compromise user devices.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: The attackers use StrongPity malware to execute malicious code on compromised devices.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attackers steal sensitive patient data for financial gain.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers disrupt healthcare services by overloading systems with traffic.

Scenario.summary: This scenario involves a simple attack chain targeting healthcare professionals with spearphishing emails and exploiting vulnerabilities in outdated software on the healthcare organization's website. The attackers then execute malware on compromised devices and steal sensitive patient data for financial gain while disrupting healthcare services.
